tenses.name: Present Simple
tenses.explanation: Use present simple for routines, habits, facts, and things that are generally true.
examples: I drink water before breakfast every day.
She teaches mathematics at a public school.
They play basketball after class on Tuesdays.
My father drives to work at 8:00 a.m.
The train leaves the station at 6:30.
We usually eat dinner together.
He does his homework before dinner.
The earth moves around the sun.
Our team meets every Monday morning.
It rains a lot in this region in winter.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: She go to the gym every morning.
She goes to the gym every morning.
She is go to the gym every morning.
She going to the gym every morning.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She goes to the gym every morning.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence expresses a general fact?
options: Water freezes at 0C.
Water is freezing right now.
Water froze last night.
Water will freeze tomorrow.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative form.
options: He don't like spicy food.
He doesn't likes spicy food.
He doesn't like spicy food.
He not like spicy food.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question form.
options: Do she work here?
Does she work here?
Is she work here?
Did she works here?

practice_questions.question: Which sentence is correct for a routine?
options: I am waking up at 6 every day.
I wake up at 6 every day.
I woke up at 6 every day.
I have wake up at 6 every day.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I wake up at 6 every day.

practice_questions.question: My sister ___ (study) English at night.
They ___ (not / watch) TV in the morning.
___ he ___ (drive) to work every day?
The shop ___ (open) at 9:00 a.m.
I ___ (have) coffee after lunch.
Correct: He walk to school every day.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He walks to school every day.

practice_questions.question: Correct: They doesn't speak French.
Correct: Does she studies at this university?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Does she study at this university?

practice_questions.question: Correct: The baby cry at night.
Correct: We goes to the market on Fridays.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We go to the market on Fridays.

tenses.name: Present Continuous
tenses.explanation: Use present continuous for actions happening now, around now, or temporary situations.
examples: I am reading an article right now.
She is preparing lunch in the kitchen.
They are studying for their final exam.
We are working on a new project this month.
He is talking to his manager on the phone.
The children are drawing pictures in class.
My friend is staying with us this week.
I am learning how to cook Thai food.
The bus is coming now.
You are improving your pronunciation quickly.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: She cooking dinner now.
She is cooking dinner now.
She is cook dinner now.
She cooks dinner now.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence describes an action happening at this moment?
options: He plays football every weekend.
He is playing football right now.
He played football yesterday.
He has played football before.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He is playing football right now.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: We not studying now.
We are not studying now.
We do not studying now.
We are not study now.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question.
options: Are they waiting for us?
Do they waiting for us?
Are they wait for us?
Did they waiting for us?

practice_questions.question: Which sentence is correct for a temporary situation?
options: I live with my cousin this month.
I am living with my cousin this month.
I lived with my cousin this month.
I have living with my cousin this month.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I am living with my cousin this month.

practice_questions.question: They ___ (watch) a movie at the moment.
I ___ (not / use) my laptop right now.
___ she ___ (study) in her room now?
The team ___ (prepare) for tomorrow's meeting.
We ___ (improve) our writing skills this semester.
Correct: He are talking to his teacher.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He is talking to his teacher.

practice_questions.question: Correct: I am read a new book now.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I am reading a new book now.

practice_questions.question: Correct: Are you writing your report now?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Are you writing your report now?

practice_questions.question: Correct: They is not listening carefully.
Correct: She not working this week.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She is not working this week.

tenses.name: Past Simple
tenses.explanation: Use past simple for completed actions at a specific time in the past.
tenses.structure: Subject + past verb (verb + ed or irregular form)
examples: I visited my grandparents last weekend.
She watched a documentary last night.
They finished the assignment yesterday.
We went to the beach on Sunday.
He wrote an email before lunch.
The class started at 9:00 a.m.
My brother bought a new phone last month.
I met your cousin at the cafe yesterday.
The company launched the app in 2024.
She did not attend the meeting yesterday.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: I goed to school yesterday.
I went to school yesterday.
I go to school yesterday.
I have gone to school yesterday.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence is in past simple?
options: She is cooking now.
She cooks every day.
She cooked dinner last night.
She has cooked dinner.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: He did not called me.
He did not call me.
He not called me.
He does not call me.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question form.
options: Did you finished your homework?
Did you finish your homework?
Do you finished your homework?
Were you finish your homework?

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct past form of 'buy'.
options: buyed
bought
buy
buying

practice_questions.question: We ___ (visit) the museum last Friday.
She ___ (not / enjoy) the movie yesterday.
___ they ___ (arrive) on time?
I ___ (see) him at the station this morning.
The teacher ___ (give) us extra homework.
Correct: They was happy after the match.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They were happy after the match.

practice_questions.question: Correct: He did not went to work yesterday.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He did not go to work yesterday.

practice_questions.question: Correct: We eated dinner at 8:00.
Correct: Did she called you last night?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Did she call you last night?

practice_questions.question: Correct: I didn't knew the answer.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I did not know the answer.

tenses.name: Past Continuous
tenses.explanation: Use past continuous for an action that was in progress at a certain time in the past.
examples: I was reading when you called me.
She was cooking dinner at 7:00 p.m.
They were studying all evening.
We were waiting for the bus in the rain.
He was driving home when the storm began.
The kids were playing in the garden.
My brother was sleeping at midnight.
I was writing my report at that time.
The team was practicing while it was snowing.
You were talking to the manager when I arrived.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: I were watching TV at 9.
I was watching TV at 9.
I was watch TV at 9.
I watching TV at 9.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence uses past continuous correctly?
options: They were play football.
They were playing football.
They played football when I call.
They have been playing football then.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: She wasn't studying last night.
She didn't studying last night.
She wasn't study last night.
She not was studying last night.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question form.
options: Were you sleeping at 11:00?
Did you sleeping at 11:00?
Was you sleeping at 11:00?
Are you sleeping at 11:00?

practice_questions.question: Choose the best sentence with an interrupted action.
options: I cooked when he arrived.
I was cooking when he arrived.
I have cooked when he arrived.
I cook when he arrived.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I was cooking when he arrived.

practice_questions.question: They ___ (wait) outside when it started raining.
He ___ (not / work) at 10 p.m. yesterday.
___ she ___ (listen) to music at that moment?
We ___ (drive) to the airport at dawn.
I ___ (review) my notes when the lights went out.
Correct: She were reading a novel at 8:00.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She was reading a novel at 8:00.

practice_questions.question: Correct: They was talking too loudly.
Correct: Were he watching the game?
Correct: I not was sleeping when you texted me.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I was not sleeping when you texted me.

practice_questions.question: Correct: We were wait for the teacher.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We were waiting for the teacher.

tenses.name: Present Perfect
tenses.explanation: Use present perfect for experiences, recent actions, and actions that connect past to now.
examples: I have visited Istanbul twice.
She has completed her assignment.
They have lived here for five years.
We have just finished lunch.
He has never tried sushi.
My parents have traveled to Spain.
I have known her since high school.
The company has launched a new service.
You have improved your grammar a lot.
It has rained three times this week.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: She have finished her work.
She has finished her work.
She has finish her work.
She finished her work already now.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence shows life experience?
options: I went to Paris in 2019.
I have been to Paris.
I was going to Paris.
I go to Paris every year.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: We haven't seen that film yet.
We didn't seen that film yet.
We not have seen that film yet.
We hasn't seen that film yet.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We haven't seen that film yet.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question form.
options: Have you ever eaten Korean food?
Did you ever eaten Korean food?
Have you ever eat Korean food?
Do you ever eaten Korean food?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Have you ever eaten Korean food?

practice_questions.question: Choose the sentence with 'since' used correctly.
options: She has worked here since three years.
She has worked here since 2021.
She worked here since 2021.
She has been work here since 2021.

practice_questions.question: I ___ (finish) my homework already.
He ___ (not / call) me yet.
___ you ever ___ (ride) a horse?
They ___ (live) in this apartment for six months.
She ___ (just / send) the email.
Correct: I has never eaten octopus.
Correct: They have went to the bank.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They have gone to the bank.

practice_questions.question: Correct: Has she finished her report yet?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Has she finished her report yet?

practice_questions.question: Correct: We didn't met him before.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We have not met him before.

practice_questions.question: Correct: He have lived here since 2015.

tenses.name: Past Perfect
tenses.explanation: Use past perfect for an action that happened before another action in the past.
examples: I had finished dinner before they arrived.
She had left the office when I called.
They had studied the chapter before the test started.
We had already seen that movie, so we chose another one.
He had never flown before his trip to Dubai.
The train had departed by the time we reached the station.
My brother had cleaned the kitchen before our guests came.
I had saved enough money before I bought the laptop.
The teacher had explained the rule before we practiced it.
She had completed the report before the deadline.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: I had ate breakfast before class.
I had eaten breakfast before class.
I have eaten breakfast before class yesterday.
I had eating breakfast before class.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I had eaten breakfast before class.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence uses past perfect correctly?
options: She had finish the task before lunch.
She had finished the task before lunch.
She finished had the task before lunch.
She has finished the task before lunch yesterday.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She had finished the task before lunch.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative form.
options: They hadn't seen that place before.
They didn't had seen that place before.
They hadn't saw that place before.
They not had seen that place before.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They hadn't seen that place before.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question.
options: Had you completed the form before the interview?
Did you had completed the form before the interview?
Had you complete the form before the interview?
Have you completed the form before the interview?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Had you completed the form before the interview?

practice_questions.question: Choose the best sentence for two past events.
options: When we arrived, the show started.
When we arrived, the show had started.
When we had arrived, the show has started.
When we arrive, the show had started.
practice_questions.correct_answer: When we arrived, the show had started.

practice_questions.question: By the time I reached the station, the train ___ (leave).
He was upset because he ___ (lose) his keys.
___ they ___ (finish) the project before the deadline?
We ___ (not / meet) each other before that day.
She ___ (already / submit) the application before noon.
Correct: They had went home before the storm.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They had gone home before the storm.

practice_questions.question: Correct: I had not knew about the change.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I had not known about the change.

practice_questions.question: Correct: Had she finished her work before she left?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Had she finished her work before she left?

practice_questions.question: Correct: We had saw that movie before.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We had seen that movie before.

practice_questions.question: Correct: He had not wrote the email when I arrived.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He had not written the email when I arrived.

tenses.name: Present Perfect Continuous
tenses.explanation: Use present perfect continuous for actions that started in the past and are still continuing, or have a visible result now.
examples: I have been studying English for three years.
She has been working at this company since 2022.
They have been waiting for the bus for twenty minutes.
We have been practicing this dialogue all morning.
He has been learning to drive since January.
It has been raining since noon.
My parents have been living in this house for a decade.
I have been reading that novel for a week.
She has been preparing for her exam since last month.
The children have been playing outside for hours.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: She has been work here for two years.
She has been working here for two years.
She is been working here for two years.
She have been working here for two years.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She has been working here for two years.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence is correct with 'for'?
options: I have been waiting since two hours.
I have been waiting for two hours.
I am waiting for two hours.
I have waited for two hours now always.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I have been waiting for two hours.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: He has not been sleeping well.
He has not sleeping well.
He have not been sleeping well.
He does not been sleeping well.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He has not been sleeping well.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question form.
options: Have you been waiting long?
Do you have been waiting long?
Have you waiting long?
Are you been waiting long?

practice_questions.question: Choose the best sentence with a present result.
options: He has been running, so he is out of breath.
He has running, so he is out of breath.
He was running, so he is out of breath now.
He run since morning, so he is out of breath.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He has been running, so he is out of breath.

practice_questions.question: I ___ (study) for the exam since 7 p.m.
It ___ (rain) all afternoon.
___ she ___ (work) here for long?
We ___ (not / sleep) enough these days.
They ___ (build) the new bridge since March.
Correct: They has been waiting for an hour.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They have been waiting for an hour.

practice_questions.question: Correct: I am been working on this task all day.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I have been working on this task all day.

practice_questions.question: Correct: Has he been studying since two weeks?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Has he been studying for two weeks?

practice_questions.question: Correct: She have been learning French for six months.
practice_questions.correct_answer: She has been learning French for six months.

practice_questions.question: Correct: We have been waited for the manager.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We have been waiting for the manager.

tenses.name: Future
tenses.explanation: Use 'will' for decisions, promises, and predictions. Use 'going to' for plans and intentions.
tenses.structure: Subject + will + base verb OR Subject + am/is/are going to + base verb
examples: I will call you after class.
She will probably arrive late.
They are going to visit their grandparents this weekend.
We are going to start a new course next week.
He will help you with your assignment.
It will rain tomorrow.
My sister is going to study medicine.
I think the team will win tonight.
The company is going to open a new office next year.
You will enjoy this lesson.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
options: I will to call you later.
I will call you later.
I am will call you later.
I going to call you later.

practice_questions.question: Which sentence shows a planned future action?
options: I think it will be sunny.
I am going to visit my aunt next month.
I will answer the phone.
I will probably be late.
practice_questions.correct_answer: I am going to visit my aunt next month.

practice_questions.question: Choose the correct negative sentence.
options: She won't attend the meeting.
She will not attends the meeting.
She isn't will attend the meeting.
She not will attend the meeting.

practice_questions.question: Pick the correct question.
options: Are you going to join us?
Do you going to join us?
Will you going to join us?
Are you go to join us?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Are you going to join us?

practice_questions.question: Choose the best sentence for a quick decision.
options: I am going to open the window.
I will open the window.
I open the window tomorrow.
I am open the window now.

practice_questions.question: They ___ (travel) to Egypt next summer.
I think he ___ (pass) the exam.
___ you ___ (help) me with this bag?
She ___ (not / come) to class tomorrow.
We ___ (meet) at the cafe at 5:00.
Correct: He will going to buy a new car.
practice_questions.correct_answer: He is going to buy a new car.

practice_questions.question: Correct: I won't to forget your birthday.
Correct: Are you going watch the game tonight?
practice_questions.correct_answer: Are you going to watch the game tonight?

practice_questions.question: Correct: They will arrives late because of traffic.
practice_questions.correct_answer: They will arrive late because of traffic.

practice_questions.question: Correct: We are go to start at nine.
practice_questions.correct_answer: We are going to start at nine.
